segments.source_start: ## The Bodhisatta [This title has been added in, as there was no overall descriptive title for this section.]
segments.source_end: ## The Bodhisatta [This title has been added in, as there was no overall descriptive title for this section.]
segments.notes: Inserted stable segment marker ahead of an existing editorial heading note.

segments.source_end: The original word in Pāli is Paṭiññā, which literally means "promise" or "vow."
segments.notes: Heading plus explanatory sentence kept as a single unit.

segments.source_start: This Pāḷi sentence is a formula of great honour paid to the Buddha
segments.source_end: A Buddhist literary work usually begins with this to show the author's devotion and obeisance.

segments.source_start: With most respectful adoration, I pay obeisance to the Buddha
segments.source_end: and who is like them in all aspects of glory, virtues and attributes.
segments.notes: Internal double spacing in raw normalized to single spaces.

segments.source_start: Malalasekera says there are eight differences (*aṭṭha-vemattāni*)
segments.source_end: [Also see the discussion in the Supplement in the Further Explanations.]
segments.notes: Embedded note marker converted to Markdown footnote [^1].

segments.source_start: With most respectful adoration, I pay obeisance to the Dhamma
segments.source_end: and which has been held in high esteem.
segments.notes: Embedded note marker converted to Markdown footnote [^2].

segments.source_start: With most respectful adoration, I pay obeisance to the Saṅgha
segments.notes: Embedded note marker converted to Markdown footnote [^3].

segments.source_start: Having paid respects to the Buddha, the Dhamma and the Saṅgha
segments.source_end: received the definite prophecy that he would become a Perfectly Self-Awakened One.
segments.notes: Two embedded note markers converted to Markdown footnotes [^4]-[^5].

segments.source_end: We shall see more about it when we come to the story of Sumedha.
segments.notes: Paragraph preserved as a single conceptual unit.

segments.source_start: May those virtuous people, who are desirous of seeking merit and knowledge;
segments.source_end: easily attain the paths, fruitions and Nibbāna.
segments.notes: Enumerated devotional blessing preserved in one segment.

segments.source_start: After the word Buddha, the author adds an adjectival clause reading:
segments.source_end: The form and formless worlds belong to the Brahmas.
segments.notes: Closing explanatory paragraph; omitted webpage footer line 'last updated: August 2024' as site metadata.
